name: testcontainers-python (Docker-Based Test Fixtures)

auth.notes: Requires Docker daemon; no external auth

performance.notes: Container startup 3-30s depending on image; subsequent tests fast

agent_readiness.notes: Spin up Docker containers for integration tests. with PostgresContainer() as pg: connect via pg.get_connection_url(). Prebuilt containers: PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, Kafka, Elasticsearch, MinIO, LocalStack, and more. pytest fixture integration. Async support (asyncpg). @pytest.fixture(scope='session') for shared containers. wait_for_logs(), wait_for_port() strategies. Python port of the Java library. Docker Compose support. Used for replacing mocks with real service containers.

gotchas: Requires Docker daemon running (CI must support Docker-in-Docker or rootless)
Container startup adds significant test time
Python port sometimes lags behind Java in features
Resource cleanup requires proper fixture scoping
best_when: Integration tests needing real databases instead of mocks
Testing code that queries PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, S3
Replacing fragile database mocks with actual service containers
avoid_when: Environments without Docker (serverless CI, some restricted environments)
summary: testcontainers-python spins up real Docker containers for integration tests. with PostgresContainer() as pg gives you a real PostgreSQL instance with connection URL. Pre-built containers for PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, MongoDB, Kafka, LocalStack. pytest fixture integration. async support. Best for replacing database mocks with real service containers in Python integration tests. Requires Docker daemon in CI environment.
